content:
Are we Roc'n the new compiler yet?

Docs

  • Richard's getting started notes for AoC
  • language reference started, but missing a lot of detail
  • examples need updating, not yet started
  • exercism track needs updating, not yet started
  • tutorial needs an update, not yet started

Roc CLI

  • roc path/to/app.roc :check: uses an interpreter for fast startup until dev backends ready
  • roc build :check: uses an interpreter shim until llvm backend ready #8780
  • roc check :check:
  • roc fmt :check:
  • roc test :check: basic implementation but usable, needs some love
  • roc repl WIP #8854
  • roc docs not yet started
  • roc glue not yet started -- required for authoring more complicated platform API's
  • roc tooling -- for building tooling for Roc using syntax or IR's

Platforms

  • Zig template :check:
  • Rust template :check:
  • basic-cli migration #413 usable if built from source
  • No other platforms migrated yet

Tooling

  • LSP available in the cli using roc experimental-lsp (needs documentation and more features)
  • Editor integrations need updating

Packages

  • Nil packages have been migrated or released yet

This is a quick snapshot of where things are at right now. Roc has been moving very quickly, so the above will probably be way out of date in a few days, but I wanted to give an overview for folks who may not be able to follow every message in Zulip.

:smiley:
Just waiting for basic-cli, packages, and JSON encode/decode, then I’m all-in :)
